name: foundation-registry
tagline: Every decision the firm has made, stored as versioned Markdown records with schemas and clear ownership.
sellMe: Firm-level decisions scatter across wikis, chat, and one-off docs, and nobody can find the reasoning six months later. This repo keeps them as Markdown-with-YAML-frontmatter records — PRDs, PDRs, ADRs, SPECs, Tasks, Releases — validated by JSON Schema and routed to named teams via CODEOWNERS. Records partition by domain (governance, design, frontend, backend, infra, security, data, observability) and cross-link forward: PRD → PDR/ADR → SPEC → Tasks → Release. It's the record of what the firm decided and why — not a runtime application.

products.name: config
products.description: Shared configs for every tool the firm uses — tsconfig base, oxlint, oxfmt, taplo, syncpack, markdownlint, yamllint, commitlint, gitleaks, editorconfig, lefthook.

products.name: core
products.description: Small runtime helpers (repoRoot, miseExec) that qa and sync both depend on.

products.name: qa
products.description: Lint, format, and structural checks across the whole tree — gitmeta, editorconfig, lefthook, and JSON-schema validation, all under one command.

products.name: sync
products.description: Drift guard for generated files — version pins, vendored schemas, Turbo config, and the post-checkout/merge hooks that keep them in step.

products.name: registry
products.description: The record spine itself — PRD, PDR, ADR, SPEC, tasks, releases — validated against a JSON Schema and rendered as a browseable index site.
